There are many readings of arachne's story but my favorite one atm is an anticapitalist, antisocial reading

Consider this. All arachne's problems stem from the fact that she has a j*b

All the emotional turmoil, dissociation, and self-flagellation could have been avoided if arachne had simply refused to work
Indeed, what is arachne's stage if not an allegory for the tyranny of social expectations and the roles we are forced to play in society???

(Aside: A spinning wheel is a fucked up gift for a little girl if you think about it. Spinning is a solitary, labor intensive form of work, which most women were forced to do for most of premodern agrarian history. The modern equivalent would be like "happy birthday jenny! For a gift, you can have your very own office cubicle, to prepare you for a life of drudgery!")
In this reading nephraea is actually a liberating but ultimately self destructive wish fulfilment figure similar to Arthur Fleck from Joker, or Tyler Durden from Fight Club
A symbol of resistance against the machine who is ultimately doomed to be ground down by it 😔
In the end, we must all follow our fate, to become empl*yed
What i am trying to say with this rant is that nephraea is actually the joker and we live in a society
